taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence Engineer
core_skills: AI/ML frameworks, Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn
skill_match_scores: AI/ML frameworks:1.0, Python:1.0, PyTorch:0.0, TensorFlow:0.0, Scikit-learn:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field. OR equivalent work experience, 7+ years in software or solution architecture; 3–5+ years in AI/ML, data science, or ML engineering, Strong hands-on foundations in Python and modern AI/ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn), Familiarity with LLM ecosystems (OpenAI, Hugging Face, LangChain or equivalents), Experience designing data pipelines, APIs, distributed systems, and feature stores, Exposure to MLOps tools and practices (CI/CD, model registry, monitoring, drift detection), Strong cloud knowledge (AWS, Azure, or GCP), Exceptionally curious, adaptive, and proactive, stays ahead of fast-changing AI technologies, Fast learner with ability to shift between conceptual and hands-on tasks, Strong problem solver with a “builder” mentality
preferred_quals: Additional certifications in AI/ML technologies are preferred
core_responsibilities: Translate business challenges into well-scoped AI solutions, balancing feasibility, value, cost, and speed, Architect end-to-end AI systems—including data ingestion, model training, inference pipelines, monitoring, and governance, Design and refine LLM/RAG architectures, agent workflows, and prompt engineering patterns, Rapidly explore emerging tools/techniques to extend AI capabilities across the organization, Build reusable reference architectures and best practices for internal teams, Partner with engineering, data science, and product teams to guide implementation, Conduct PoCs, prototypes, and pilots to validate technical suitability before scaling, Ensure solutions meet performance, security, compliance, and cost-efficiency requirements, Integrate AI capabilities into existing systems, both cloud and legacy, Work with MLOps/DevOps to establish robust CI/CD, observability, and lifecycle management
job_summary: Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. The AI Solutions Architect will translate business challenges into AI solutions, architect end-to-end AI systems, and partner with engineering and product teams to guide implementation.
